subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, EC, Eurostat, ECB, WHO/OECD, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Bulgarian official (NSI, Bulgarian National Bank) + Bulgarian/regional press (Novinite, Balkan Insight, OSW).
Joined the euro on 2026-01-01 (ending the currency board) and full Schengen in 2025; quietly positive macro fundamentals offset by institutional, demographic, and health weaknesses.

Standout drags: the EU's weakest rule-of-law/corruption record, chronic political instability, the world's fastest-shrinking population, and poor health outcomes; low debt and euro entry are the offsets. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone.

economic.notes: Very low debt, euro entry, and diversified exports - the quiet strength of the profile.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~30% GDP (among the EU's lowest), though the 2025 deficit (~3.5%) breached the 3% mark; currency-board-disciplined. (Eurostat; IMF Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A currency board now succeeded by euro adoption (Jan 2026) - policy ECB-pooled, the central bank on the Governing Council. (ECB; BNB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Now a euro member, gaining shared reserve-currency status. (ECB)

inflation.notes: Eased to ~2.7% to meet the euro criterion, with some post-changeover pass-through risk. (EC/ECB convergence reports)

banking_stability.notes: Sound, with strong credit growth, now Eurosystem-supervised. (IMF Art IV; BNB)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Diversified exports (electronics, a large ICT sector), EU single-market and nearshoring; no resource curse. (EC; trade.gov)

institutional.notes: Credible statistics, against the EU's weakest rule-of-law record and oligarchic capture.

rule_of_law.notes: Near the EU's bottom - oligarchic capture and near-zero high-level corruption convictions; EU-monitored reform only. (TI CPI; EC Rule of Law Report)

statistical_integrity.notes: NSI is Eurostat-compliant and usable. (Eurostat; NSI)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Politicised, with deadlocked appointments and some recovery-fund-driven digitalisation. (EC; BTI 2026)

press_freedom.notes: Recovered from a near-bottom EU standing but slipped again (RSF ~70th), with SLAPPs and ownership opacity. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Chronic political churn and the world's fastest-shrinking population, against strong EU-standard liberties.

political_stability.notes: Around eight elections in five years, with governments resigning amid anti-corruption protests - democratic but chronically unstable. (Wilson Center; BTI 2026)

civil_liberties.notes: EU-standard and generally strong. (V-Dem/Freedom House class)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: An EU member in full Schengen with lawful routes; emigration-heavy with modest immigration. (EC)

social_cohesion.notes: Polarised, with Roma exclusion and anti-corruption and pro/anti-Russia cleavages. (BTI; OSW)

demographics.notes: Among the world's fastest-shrinking populations - deaths roughly twice births, heavy emigration, and deep ageing. (NSI; UN/OSW)

geopolitical.notes: A net Western-aligned NATO/EU member, with a persistent Russia-sympathetic strand and Black Sea adjacency.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO and EU member and Black Sea frontline, net Western-aligned, with a persistent Russia-sympathetic strand. (multilateral)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active war, but Ukraine/Black-Sea-adjacent spillover risk. (press)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open EU regime with free capital movement, now euro. (EC)

physical_practical.notes: Improving EU-funded infrastructure, against the EU's worst health outcomes and a coal-transition lag.

climate.notes: SE-Europe heat/drought and Black-Sea/agro water stress, the lowest-readiness EU member, markedly worse by mid-century. (ND-GAIN; EEA)

healthcare.notes: The EU's lowest per-capita spend and highest treatable mortality, with heavy medic emigration. (WHO/OECD)

infrastructure.notes: Historically poor, improving via EU/recovery funds. (EC; OECD)

crime_safety.notes: Low homicide but entrenched organized-crime/trafficking transit. (OC Index 2025)

disaster_insurance.notes: Medium seismic (including Sofia) and flood exposure against low penetration. (World Bank)

summary: An EU member with quietly positive economic fundamentals - among the bloc's lowest debt, euro adoption from January 2026, sound banks, and diversified exports - whose integrated outlook is pulled down by institutional, demographic, and health weaknesses. It has the EU's weakest rule-of-law and corruption record under oligarchic capture, chronic political instability (around eight elections in five years), among the world's fastest-shrinking populations, and the EU's poorest health outcomes. NATO/EU membership anchors it westward despite a persistent Russia-sympathetic strand.
